digestSummary: This week saw a landmark High Court decision on separation of powers (Smith v Jones) and a significant procedural fairness ruling (Brown v State of NSW).

topCases.factsSummary: Challenge to legislation conferring judicial power on non-judicial body.
topCases.legalAnalysis: Court held functions inherently judicial; conferral on non-Ch III body invalid.

topCases.factsSummary: Challenge to administrative decision on procedural fairness grounds.
topCases.legalAnalysis: Court reinforced that procedural fairness requires opportunity to be heard.
topCases.whyItMatters: Confirms high bar for denying hearing rights.

extendedCases.oneLineSummary: NSW Court of Appeal upheld damages assessment methodology for breach of commercial lease.
